text: most of Cheyenne’s humans, they were still her people, and they’d all been forced into lives of fear by Warlord. She’d grown up around them, knew their faces, had traded with them. She’d recognized the suffering in the eyes of the children running through the filthy streets. It was the same suffering she’d known her whole life . And all she could do was run away. She hated feeling so helpless. Hated that there was nothing they could do. It was wrong, but what choice did they have ? “You’re right,” she whispered. “We can’t go back .” “It may be difficult to believe,” Ronin said, placing a warm hand under her chin and guiding her eyes to his, “but there is an entire world beyond Cheyenne. We can make a future somewhere, and this may be the only chance we have to do so together .” Lara nodded. However terrible the situation was in Cheyenne, she had something to live for. Someone . Mental exhaustion tumbled atop her, weighing her down; this was too much, too quickly. She sagged into Ronin’s embrace, resting her head on his shoulder . Newton said something quietly, but she’d already closed her eyes and stopped paying attention . Once the storm was over and they left this place, she’d see the world, and she was glad Ronin would be the one to show her. However, there would always be a patch of gloom overshadowing their happiness, knowing she’d done nothing to help the others . Chapter Twenty - Six R onin brushed his fingers over Lara’s cheek and watched her lashes flutter open. She stretched against him and released a long, slow breath. Turning toward him, she stared up through the darkness, pupils dilated . “Is it time to go?” she rasped . “After you have something to eat and drink .” “ Kay .” Lara pulled herself out of bed with a soft grunt. Ronin moved ahead of her, lighting the lantern. She raised her hand to shield her eyes from the light, and yawned into the crook of her elbow . “How long was I asleep ?” “Five hours and twelve minutes. The sun should be up by now .” “How long did the storm last?” She found her canteen and took a long drink . “We last heard it a little more than three hours ago,” Newton replied from his seat on the steps. “It was one of the more tenacious storms in recent memory .” Ronin checked the straps and ties on their bags. He’d wanted to be days away from Cheyenne before making such a long stop, but the Dust didn’t care about anyone’s plans. They had miles to make up; Warlord was far too close for Ronin’s liking . “The toilet is through there,” Newton said, gesturing to the closed door near Lara. “I’ve kept it in working condition, for lack of anything better to do .” “Seriously? I’ve been holding it this whole time only for you to tell me you have a toilet now ?” “My apologies. Given the nature of our earlier conversations, there seemed no prudent opportunities to inform you .” “Any time is a good time to tell someone there’s a toilet.” She smirked before rushing into the room and closing the door behind her . “I will have to keep that in mind for future encounters,” Newton mused. “She is uniquely spirited, Ronin .” You should see her dance . He stopped himself from saying it aloud. Lara’s dancing, Ronin decided, was for him. “Learned more about being alive in a few weeks with her than I have since you reactivated me .” “Would that couplings such as yours were the rule, rather than the exception to it .” Ronin shrugged. He spread a cloth on the floor and laid his rifle atop it, disassembling it rapidly. “Doesn’t make much difference what anyone else says or does. The world’s approval doesn’t matter to us .” “You would face deactivation for her, if it came down to it?” There was a strange hesitance in Newton’s voice . “Yes. I’ve faced it over things far less important to me than she is .” The door opened, and Lara emerged. Her face was scrubbed clean, cheeks pink, and she’d wrapped her scarf over her hair . “You have no idea how much better I feel, now.” She moved to her pack, closed it, and swung it up over her shoulders. “I’ll eat on the way .” “All right.” Ronin inspected the rifle’s parts closely. They’d made it out of the storm before much dust had built up inside of it, so he cleaned the pieces quickly and put them back together. That done, he stood up and pulled on the other bags . Lara approached Newton. He stood as she neared . “Thank you. For letting us stay .” “It is the least I could do,” Newton replied, lip plates lifting into a smile. “Especially considering you are the ones with the firearms .” She returned his smile, though her expression slipped a moment later. “I’m…sorry. For what I said. I know there isn’t — ” “You do not need to apologize to me. You’ve every right to be angry about what’s happened, and there’s nothing we can say or do to erase those wrongs. I wish we’d met under more pleasant circumstances, Miss Brooks, but I am glad to have met you, all the same .” “Me too,” she said. “Maybe…you could come with us? I mean, this place is better than my old shack, but…you could do better .” “Perhaps the next time you stumble in unannounced, I will accept the invitation,” he replied, a sad humor in his voice. “I don’t think I’m quite ready to lift my self-imposed exile, but you’ve given me much to think on .” “Well, then, I guess…until next time ?” Newton stood aside to allow her up the stairs .
